message:
feat(scanner): add cass_*, frankentui-*, and *_target patterns

Closes the disk-pressure pattern gap that just filled /tmp with 211 GB
of leftover agent dirs. Three new artifact-pattern families and one
suffix matcher in the scanner registry, plus matching label entries
for stats grouping.

New prefix patterns (Prefix matcher, AgentWorkspace category):
- cass-prefix-underscore (cass_*)        confidence 0.86
- cass-prefix-hyphen     (cass-*)        confidence 0.86
- frankentui-prefix-hyphen (frankentui-*) confidence 0.90
- frankentui-prefix-underscore (frankentui_*) confidence 0.90

New suffix pattern (Suffix matcher, RustTarget category):
- underscore-target-suffix (*_target)    confidence 0.92

Confidence rationale: the *_target suffix is intentionally above the
agent-prefix confidences so a name like cass_append_baseline_target
(matches BOTH cass_ and _target) classifies as the more specific
RustTarget. Both categories lead to deletion; the distinction matters
for stats and for the structural-score boost RustTarget gets when
cargo markers are present.

extract_pattern_label() updated with matching ordering: *_target
checked before cass_*/cass-* so the same name groups consistently
in stats reports.

Five new unit tests cover:
- 7 real cass_* dirs from the just-cleaned /tmp set
- 7 real frankentui-* dirs (codex bead workspaces, profile data)
- *_target suffix on agent-prefixed cargo dirs
- frankentui- vs frankenterm- collision check
- extract_pattern_label grouping for all new families

All 1870 lib tests pass; cargo fmt clean.

Wider context: dcg recently shipped the find -delete bypass closure,
which surfaced this gap when /tmp filled to 100% (250 GB tmpfs) from
prior agent sessions and ANY hook fork() started failing with ENOSPC.
sbh's scanner is the right place for this — dcg is a pre-execution
guard, sbh is the cleanup layer.
